chapters.title: Keyumars — The First King
chapters.summary: From the mountains of Pars, Keyumars rules a world still soft with dawn. He clothes himself in leopard skin and the beasts bow.
chapters.story: From the mountains of Pars, Keyumars ruled a world still soft with dawn. He clothed himself in leopard skin, and the beasts of the field came down to bow at his feet. His son Siyamak fell to the demon Khazura, and Keyumars wept thirty days for the loss. The first kingdom was forged in mourning and song.
chapters.story_fa: از کوه‌های پارس، کیومرث بر جهانی فرمان راند که هنوز در سپیده‌دم نرم بود. پوست پلنگ بر تن کرد و چارپایان دشت در پای او سر فرود آوردند. پسرش سیامک به دست دیو خزروانی افتاد و کیومرث سی روز در سوگ نشست.

chapters.title: Hushang — The Spark of Fire
chapters.summary: Hushang strikes flint at a serpent and instead wakes the eternal flame. Sade, the festival of fire, is born.
chapters.story: Hushang the wise grandson struck flint at a serpent of black scales. The stone missed, but a spark leapt up and a tree caught fire. From that night the people knew flame, and Hushang named the festival Sade. Iron was forged. Water was led to fields. The first crafts were taught to humankind.
chapters.story_fa: هوشنگ، نوه خردمند، چخماق بر افعی سیاه‌فلس فرود آورد. سنگ خطا کرد، اما جرقه‌ای جهید و درختی شعله‌ور شد. از آن شب مردم آتش را شناختند و هوشنگ جشن سده را نام نهاد.

chapters.title: Tahmuras — Binder of Demons
chapters.summary: Tahmuras the Div-band rides through smoke and chains the demons. They beg for life and trade their secret: writing.
chapters.story: Tahmuras the Div-band rode through smoke and shadow with a club of cypress. He chained the demons and dragged them to the foot of his throne. To save their lives the demons taught him thirty alphabets — Greek, Tazi, Pahlavi, Sogdian. Knowledge was bought with mercy, and the world remembered.
chapters.story_fa: تهمورث دیوبند با گرز سرو از میان دود و سایه گذشت. دیوان را به زنجیر کشید و به پای تخت آورد. دیوان برای رهایی، سی دبیره به او آموختند.

chapters.title: Jamshid — The Golden Throne
chapters.summary: Jamshid divides the people into four crafts and sits a kingdom of crystal. Pride finally outshines the sun.
chapters.story: Jamshid divided the people into four crafts: priests, warriors, farmers, artisans. He raised a crystal throne and was carried into the sky by demons on Nowruz. Three hundred years of light. Then pride: he claimed to be a god, and the divine grace, the farr, departed from him.
chapters.story_fa: جمشید مردم را به چهار پیشه کرد: موبدان، جنگاوران، کشاورزان و پیشه‌وران. تخت بلورین برافراشت و دیوان او را در نوروز به آسمان بردند. سپس کبر آورد و فره از او دور شد.

chapters.title: Zahhak — The Serpent King
chapters.summary: Two serpents grow from Zahhak's shoulders. Their daily price is two human brains. A thousand years of darkness.
chapters.story: From the kiss of Iblis, two black serpents sprang from Zahhak's shoulders. Each day demanded two human brains, drawn from the youth of Pars. A thousand years of darkness pressed on the land — until a blacksmith's apron rose as a banner.
chapters.story_fa: از بوسه ابلیس، دو مار سیاه بر دوش ضحاک رویید. هر روز دو مغز جوان می‌خواست. هزار سال تاریکی، تا روزی که پیش‌بند کاوه آهنگر بر سر نیزه شد.

chapters.title: Fereydun — The Liberator
chapters.summary: Kaveh the blacksmith raises his apron as a banner. Fereydun's bull-headed mace ends a tyrant.
chapters.story: Kaveh the blacksmith raised his leather apron on a spear, and the people of Pars marched. Fereydun took up the bull-headed mace forged for this hour. Zahhak was bound to Damavand's mountain forever. The kingdom split among Fereydun's three sons: Iraj, Tur, Salm — and from their feud, the Shahnameh's wars began.
chapters.story_fa: کاوه آهنگر پیش‌بند چرمی را بر نیزه برافراشت. فریدون گرز گاوسر برداشت. ضحاک تا ابد بر دماوند به بند کشیده شد.

chapters.title: Zal — The Albino Prince
chapters.summary: Born white-haired, cast to the mountain, raised by Simorgh, Zal returns to Pars on wings of myth.
chapters.story: Born white-haired, the prince Zal was cast onto the mountain by his father Sam, who feared the omen. The Simorgh found him and raised him with her chicks for years. When Sam repented and climbed the mountain, the Simorgh gave Zal back, and one feather, to be burned in greatest need.
chapters.story_fa: زال، شاهزاده سپیدموی، بر کوه رها شد. سیمرغ او را یافت و با جوجگانش پرورد. سام پشیمان از کوه بالا رفت و سیمرغ زال را بازگرداند، با پری برای بدترین روز.

chapters.title: Rostam — Pahlavan of the Age
chapters.summary: Seven labours, one Rakhsh, no equal under the sky. Rostam becomes the world's pillar of arms.
chapters.story: Seven labours: a lion in the night, a desert without water, a dragon, a witch, a div-king Akvan, the demon king of Mazandaran. Rakhsh chose his rider himself. From that day Rostam was Iran's pillar of arms — and his shadow stretched over five generations of kings.
chapters.story_fa: هفت خوان: شیر در شب، بیابان بی‌آب، اژدها، جادوگر، اکوان دیو، دیو سپید مازندران. رخش خود سوار خویش را برگزید.

chapters.title: Sohrab — Son of the Storm
chapters.summary: Two armies camp on opposite shores. Father and son, unknowing, raise blades against each other.
chapters.story: From Tahmineh's chamber, Sohrab grew tall as a cedar. He led the army of Turan into Iran, searching for the father he had never seen. Father and son met on the battlefield, neither speaking their name. Rostam's blade fell. The bracelet on Sohrab's arm answered every question too late.
chapters.story_fa: سهراب از خوابگاه تهمینه به قامت سرو بالید. لشکر توران را به ایران آورد، در جست‌وجوی پدری که نمی‌شناخت. پدر و پسر در میدان رویاروی شدند و بازوبند سهراب بسیار دیر سخن گفت.

chapters.title: Esfandiyar — The Brazen-Bodied
chapters.summary: Steel cannot cut him, except where his eyes can be reached.
chapters.story: Esfandiyar was bathed in fire by the prophet Zoroaster, and his body became brazen. No iron could harm him — except his eyes, kept shut at the moment of immersion. His seven labours rivalled Rostam's. His ending arrived in a hail of feathers and arrows under the cypress.
chapters.story_fa: اسفندیار به دست زرتشت در آتش غسل یافت و تن او رویین شد. هیچ آهنی نمی‌توانست او را بیازارد، مگر چشمانش که در لحظه فرو رفتن بسته بود.

chapters.title: Simorgh — The Phoenix Council
chapters.summary: Wings the colour of the sun. The mountain remembers a wisdom older than language.
chapters.story: Wings the colour of the sun. The Simorgh's nest holds wisdom older than the first speech. She watches the line of Zal across generations, and burns the feather only when the world's last hope hangs by a thread.
chapters.story_fa: بال‌هایی به رنگ خورشید. آشیانه سیمرغ خرد را پیش از نخستین سخن نگاه می‌دارد.

chapters.title: The Final War — Ages End
chapters.summary: The chronicle closes where it began: with fire, with iron, with kings.
chapters.story: The chronicle closes where it began: with fire, with iron, with kings. Ferdowsi's verses outlive the empires they describe. What was once a kingdom of crystal becomes a kingdom of memory, and every reader joins the lineage of Pars.
chapters.story_fa: این داستان آنجا که آغاز شد، پایان می‌گیرد: با آتش، با آهن، با شاهان. سخن فردوسی از شاهنشاهی‌هایی که وصف می‌کند جان‌سخت‌تر است.
